description: Mutation + transaction behaviour (DELETE/CHANGE NODE, nested BEGIN/ROLLBACK) on a throwaway rw worktree off forgeql-pub.frozen. node_ids are captured at runtime, never hard-coded.

steps.fql: FIND symbols IN 'crates/forgeql-core/src/node_id.rs' WHERE fql_kind='function' WHERE name='parse_offset' LIMIT 1
FIND symbols IN 'crates/forgeql-core/src/node_id.rs' WHERE fql_kind='function' WHERE name='parse_offset' LIMIT 1
FIND symbols IN 'crates/forgeql-core/src/node_id.rs' WHERE fql_kind='function' WHERE name='parse_offset' LIMIT 1
FIND symbols IN 'crates/forgeql-core/src/node_id.rs' WHERE fql_kind='function' WHERE name='parse_offset' LIMIT 1
CHANGE NODE '${A}' IF REV '${R}' WITH 'fn parse_offset_renamed() {}'
FIND symbols IN 'crates/forgeql-core/src/node_id.rs' WHERE fql_kind='function' WHERE name='parse_offset' LIMIT 1
FIND symbols IN 'crates/forgeql-core/src/node_id.rs' WHERE fql_kind='function' WHERE name='parse_offset' LIMIT 1
FIND symbols IN 'crates/forgeql-core/src/node_id.rs' WHERE fql_kind='function' WHERE name='parse_offset' LIMIT 1
FIND symbols IN 'crates/forgeql-core/src/node_id.rs' WHERE fql_kind='function' WHERE name='parse_offset' LIMIT 1
FIND symbols IN 'crates/forgeql-core/src/node_id.rs' WHERE fql_kind='function' WHERE name='offset_lines' LIMIT 1
FIND symbols IN 'crates/forgeql-core/src/node_id.rs' WHERE fql_kind='function' WHERE name='offset_lines' LIMIT 1
FIND symbols IN 'crates/forgeql-core/src/node_id.rs' WHERE fql_kind='function' WHERE name='offset_lines' LIMIT 1
FIND symbols IN 'crates/forgeql-core/src/node_id.rs' WHERE fql_kind='function' WHERE name='parse_offset' LIMIT 1
FIND symbols IN 'crates/forgeql-core/src/node_id.rs' WHERE fql_kind='function' WHERE name='parse_offset' LIMIT 1

cases.name: member_and_outline_rows_carry_a_usable_handle_and_rev
cases.description: A SHOW members / SHOW outline row is a row you can mutate FROM: it prints node_id and rev together, and that pair is exactly what the mandatory IF REV gate accepts. The member handle is attached by BYTE CONTAINMENT, not by line equality: SHOW members reports SymbolMatch::count at line 157, but the indexed field node starts at 156 — its #[serde(...)] attribute is part of the node — so a line lookup finds nothing. This case is the only thing that can catch that regression; the in-process engine_integration harness runs the legacy AST path, which has no revs and no byte lookup, so it cannot observe either field.

steps.fql: CHANGE NODE '${M}' IF REV 'hdeadbeefdeadbeef' WITH '    pub count: Option<usize>,'
CHANGE NODE '${M}' IF REV '${RM}' WITH '    pub member_handle_worked: Option<usize>,'
CHANGE NODE '${O}' IF REV '${RO}' WITH 'fn outline_handle_worked() {}'

cases.name: deleted_twin_handle_dies_instead_of_repointing
cases.description: Two byte-identical same-parent comments share every remapper discriminator AND the same rev. Pre-fix, deleting the first re-keyed the survivor onto the deleted ordinal, so the stale handle+rev pair kept resolving — the one IF REV blind spot. The tombstone keeps the survivor on its own ordinal: the stale handle must now fail.

steps.fql: FIND symbols IN 'zz_twin_probe.rs' WHERE fql_kind = 'comment' ORDER BY line ASC LIMIT 5
CHANGE NODE '${T1}' IF REV '${R1}' WITH '// hijacked'
FIND symbols IN 'zz_twin_probe.rs' WHERE fql_kind = 'comment' ORDER BY line ASC LIMIT 5
